name: Cucidata
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind figs, dates, raisins, walnuts, and orange together.
2. Mix with sugars, cinnamon, and preserves. Set aside.
3. Beat eggs well. Add vanilla and milk. Beat until combined.
4. Mix sugar and shortening together. Gradually add to eggs.
5. Continue beating.
6. Mix 1 cup flour with baking powder and salt.
7. Continue adding flour until a soft dough forms.
8. Knead gently.
9. Divide into 6 parts. Form a roll no more than 1 inch thick.
10. Flatten with a rolling pin on a slightly floured board to about 2 inches wide.
11. Spread filling down middle of dough.
12. Fold one side over filling, then the other, overlapping about 1/4 inch.
13. Seal lightly.
14. Cut diagonally into 2-inch lengths.
15. Continue with remaining dough and filling.
16. Place seam side down on a lightly greased cookie sheet.
17. Bake at 375°F for about 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
18. Cool, frost with icing, and decorate with colored sugars.
19. Makes about 10 dozen cookies (120 cookies; 20 per pound).
